title: Find Median from Data Stream
description: You are given a data stream that can be represented as a sequence of numbers. Your task is to find the median of all elements in the stream after each insertion. The median is defined as the middle value in the ordered list of numbers. If there is an even number of elements, it is the average of the two middle values.

examples.explanation: After inserting elements 1, 2, 3, 4, and 5, the sorted list is [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]. The median is the middle element, which is 3.
Inserting elements 2, 3, 4, 5, and 1 gives the sorted list [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]. The median remains 3 as it's still the middle element.
The sorted insertion of elements 5, 15, 10, 20, and 30 results in [5, 10, 15, 20, 30]. The median here is the middle value, which is 15.

constraints: The input stream will contain at most 10^5 elements.
Each element will be a valid integer.
hints: Consider using two heaps to maintain the lower and upper halves of the numbers.
To calculate the median efficiently, balance the sizes of both heaps.
Think about edge cases where the total number of elements is even or odd.
Look into how the insertion of an element affects the median calculation.
